title: Fix incorrect multipler for limit field
body:
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

# Pull Request Checklist

<!-- Please keep this section. It will make the maintainer's life easier. -->

- [ ] Privileged queries and mutations are either absent or guarded by proper permission checks
- [ ] Database queries are optimized and the number of queries is constant
- [ ] Database migrations are either absent or optimized for zero downtime
- [ ] The changes are covered by test cases
- [ ] All new fields/inputs/mutations have proper labels added (`ADDED_IN_X`, `PREVIEW_FEATURE`, etc.)
- [ ] All migrations have proper dependencies
- [ ] All indexes are added concurrently in migrations
- [ ] All RunSql and RunPython migrations have revert option defined

steps.operation: Fix query cost multipliers for Attribute.values and SelectedAttribute.values by moving the `limit` multiplier from SelectedAttribute.values to Attribute.values
steps.reason: The `values` field was recently introduced with an input `limit`, and the query cost multiplier was accidentally applied to `SelectedAttribute.values` (which is not paginated and does not expose `limit`). The multiplier must be applied where `limit` is actually accepted (on `Attribute.values`) so complexity scales correctly with requested amount of data.
steps.side_effects: GraphQL query complexity calculation will change: queries using `Attribute.values(limit: ...)` will now properly scale cost with `limit`, while `SelectedAttribute.values` will become cheaper (no multiplier). This can affect rate limiting/throttling behavior, query rejection/acceptance at max complexity thresholds, and potentially tests or monitoring dashboards that assert specific complexity scores.

step_plan.summary: Adjusts the GraphQL query cost map so the `limit`-based multiplier applies to `Attribute.values` (where `limit` exists) instead of `SelectedAttribute.values` (where it does not), correcting complexity calculation for the new limited values field.
